title: pi-workflows has no aggregate token-budget surface — each step tracks per-step usage but no WorkflowSpec-level budget.maxTokens / budget.spent() / budget.remaining() across all steps; cannot express budget-aware termination or fail-on-overspend semantics
description: Per R-0014 recon axis 7: Claude Code workflows expose budget.total / budget.spent() / budget.remaining() globals shared across the main script + all spawned agents; decrements as agents run; HARD ceiling — agent() throws once total reached. pi-workflows has per-step usage tracking (input/output tokens, cache, cost via dispatch.ts) but no aggregate across steps + no enforcement at the WorkflowSpec level. Operators cannot cap runaway loops by budget; cannot route by remaining-budget (e.g. fast-path when low; thorough-path when ample); cannot fail-with-structured-error on overspend.

evidence.reference: Part 3 axis 7 — Token Budget Tracking — ADOPT verdict
per-step usage capture; no aggregate sink

impact: Runaway loops + accidental overspend on long-running workflows are not contained at the framework layer. Operators must manually track aggregate spend via post-hoc inspection of per-step usage. Budget-aware termination (loop-until-budget-remaining; see FGAP-140) cannot be implemented without this surface. Composability with Claude Code workflows' budget model (when pi-workflows are invoked from Claude-Code-side) is poor — the two budget models don't speak the same language.
canonical_vocabulary: workflow-level token budget; budget.maxTokens; budget.spent; budget.remaining; budget-aware termination; aggregate usage sink
proposed_resolution: Extend WorkflowSpec with optional budget object: { maxTokens?: number, maxCost?: number, alertThreshold?: number (0-1 fraction; default 0.8) }. Extend ExecutionState with budgetSpent: { tokens: number, cost: number, byStep: Record<string, {tokens, cost}> }. Executor accumulates per-step usage into budgetSpent after each step completes. Pre-step check: if budget exhausted → throw BudgetExhaustedError with structured payload. Expose to expression scope as budget.spent / budget.remaining for use in transform / gate / until predicates. Updates: types.ts (WorkflowSpec + ExecutionState), workflow-executor.ts (accumulation + check), expression.ts (budget. scope).
